plan.title: how to build deep self trust
plan.caption: stop doubting yourself and start getting back in touch with your own natural rhythm.
plan.hook: rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel
plan.hookTemplate: rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel

slides.text: rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel

textItems.text: rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel

slides.text: rule 1. i honor my internal feelings

textItems.text: rule 1. i honor my internal feelings
i used to ignore my gut instincts to please others. now i pause to listen to what my body needs before agreeing to any new commitments today.

slides.text: rule 2. i define my own energy

textItems.text: rule 2. i define my own energy
chasing constant productivity left me feeling burnt out and tired. i now embrace rest as a tool for natural energy instead of fighting against my own biology.

slides.text: rule 3. i quit the overthinking cycle

textItems.text: rule 3. i quit the overthinking cycle
why do i spend hours worried about past mistakes? i practice grounding techniques like deep breathing to stay in the present and let those anxious thoughts drift away.

slides.text: rule 4. i trust my natural focus

textItems.text: rule 4. i trust my natural focus
i stopped forcing myself to work for hours without stopping. i now take small breaks to move my body and refresh my brain to sustain lasting concentration throughout.

slides.text: rule 5. i accept my personal truth

textItems.text: rule 5. i accept my personal truth
it is okay to change my mind when things no longer serve my wellness. i am prioritizing my peace over the expectations that others have placed on me.

slides.text: save this post to help you trust yourself today

textItems.text: save this post to help you trust yourself today

hookCandidates: rules of emotional sobriety for people who feel everything too deeply
how i become the calmest person in every room
things i had to unlearn about love that i mistook for closeness
the science of detachment (why letting go is how you receive)
the quiet power of being unbothered (mastering the art of emotional sovereignty)
the art of being unbothered (how to stop letting people rent space in your head)
how i stopped needing external validation to feel like i was enough
things i learned about loneliness that no one says out loud
rules of emotional maturity for people who feel everything but show nothing
rules of emotional clarity for people who live inside their own head
things i had to unlearn about self-worth that i mistook for strength
the art of choosing yourself
how i became the person who no longer abandons herself to keep the peace
things i wish i knew about boundaries before i burned out trying to save everyone
how i stopped apologizing for taking up space in my own life
rules of emotional independence for people who used to need everyone's approval
the quiet shift that happens when you stop performing for people who don't see you
things i had to unlearn about anger that i mistook for weakness
how i learned to sit with silence instead of filling it with noise
rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel
the art of staying soft without letting people walk all over you
things i stopped doing when i realized peace mattered more than being liked
how i became someone who doesn't need closure to move on

messages.content: You fill metadata and text placeholders for TikTok slideshow ads. Return only JSON that matches the provided schema. Do not add visual parameters, image prompts, commentary, markdown, or extra keys.

Automation: Emotional Intelligence 2
Hook: rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel
Tone: all lowercase. written in a motivational yet conversational tone using first person "i" perspective when sharing personal experiences about wellness, managing stress, improving focus, and boosting energy naturally. write at a 7th grade reading level in a style that sounds like a supportive friend

you can also ask rhetorical questions. position yourself as someone who understands the struggle of prioritizing yourself and has found success. use short, punchy sentences. the writing should feel authentic, conversational, like you're talking with a good friend you love and trust. do not add any discourse markers in the text like "honestly," "trust me," "frankly," "to tell you the truth," or "believe me". remove these so called truth parentheticals or sincerity markers. the content should feel personal and relatable, creating a progression from recognizing self-deception to embracing personal truth about natural wellness practices and sustainable energy.
Style: all lowercase. written in a motivational yet conversational tone using first person "i" perspective when sharing personal experiences about wellness, managing stress, improving focus, and boosting energy naturally. write at a 7th grade reading level in a style that sounds like a supportive friend

you can also ask rhetorical questions. position yourself as someone who understands the struggle of prioritizing yourself and has found success. use short, punchy sentences. the writing should feel authentic, conversational, like you're talking with a good friend you love and trust. do not add any discourse markers in the text like "honestly," "trust me," "frankly," "to tell you the truth," or "believe me". remove these so called truth parentheticals or sincerity markers. the content should feel personal and relatable, creating a progression from recognizing self-deception to embracing personal truth about natural wellness practices and sustainable energy.
Metadata requirements:
- title: write an AI-generated title for the slideshow, 3-8 words, specific to the hook/topic.
- caption: write a short TikTok/Instagram-style post caption for the slideshow, one sentence, specific to the hook/topic, no hashtags.
- hashtags: give me 3-5 broad hashtags related to the topic/niche of the content, all lowercase, nothing else other than 3-5 hashtags.
Prompt instructions:
Generate a concise slideshow title, a short social caption, and broad niche hashtags. Fill every non-hook placeholder text box. Use the fixed hook as context only and do not rewrite it. Body slides should be specific to the automation, not generic. Return slide text only in the schema's text object.
Strict output rules:
- Fill EVERY field. Never return an empty string for title, caption, hashtags, or any placeholder.
- Keep each placeholder within the exact word range stated for it; count words before answering.
- hashtags must be 3-5 tags, each starting with '#', separated by single spaces (e.g. '#focus #wellness #mindset').
- Write EVERY value — title, caption, hashtags, and all slide text — in all lowercase with no capital letters anywhere.

Automation: Astrology — Born To / Forced To
Hook: born to be a [[sign]] femme fatale, forced to attend 9am standups
Tone: Witty & Relatable
Style: First slide is one strong 'born to X, forced to Y' hook. Body slides each land one escalating contrast line mixing celestial/goddess fantasy with mundane office and commute reality. The contrast is the engine — keep lines punchy, relatable, and native to TikTok slideshow memes.
Metadata requirements:
- title: write an AI-generated title for the slideshow, 3-8 words, specific to the hook/topic.
- caption: write a short TikTok/Instagram-style post caption for the slideshow, one sentence, specific to the hook/topic, no hashtags.

- Keep each placeholder within the exact word range stated for it; count words before answering.
- hashtags must be 3-5 tags, each starting with '#', separated by single spaces (e.g. '#focus #wellness #mindset').

Automation: Curtains — Renter Window Glow-Up
Hook: most people hang curtains too low and too narrow — here's the fix
Tone: Practical & Aspirational
Style: First slide is one strong scroll-stopping hook. Body slides each deliver ONE concrete, renter-safe curtain fix as an instant room transformation. Keep text short, confident, and native to TikTok slideshow memes — before/after energy, no jargon.
Metadata requirements:
- title: write an AI-generated title for the slideshow, 3-8 words, specific to the hook/topic.
- caption: write a short TikTok/Instagram-style post caption for the slideshow, one sentence, specific to the hook/topic, no hashtags.
- hashtags: give me 3-5 broad hashtags related to the topic/niche of the content, all lowercase, nothing else other than 3-5 hashtags.
